projects.name: Mess food feedback analyzer
projects.description:
Mess Food Feedback Analyzer is a machine learning-based project developed using Python to analyze and predict mess food ratings based on various factors such as food quality, cleanliness, quantity, and taste. The project aims to help understand student satisfaction by converting feedback data into meaningful insights through data analysis and prediction models.
The system uses libraries like Pandas, NumPy, Matplotlib, and Scikitlearn for data processing, visualization, and model training. Linear Regression is implemented to predict overall food ratings based on user input parameters. The project also includes data preprocessing, feature selection, and visualization techniques to improve analysis and understand trends in feedback data.

This project demonstrates practical applications of machine learning, data analysis, and predictive modeling in solving real-world problems. It also reflects skills in Python programming, handling datasets, and building beginner-friendly AI/ML solutions.

gh.description: A machine learning project that analyzes and predicts mess food ratings based on quality, cleanliness, quantity, and taste using Python and Linear Regression.

projects.name: QuantNova
projects.description:
QuantNova is an open-source GUI-based quantitative backtesting platform designed to make algorithmic trading workflows more accessible, visual, and contributor-friendly. The project bridges the gap between code-heavy frameworks like Backtrader and closed platforms like TradingView by combining modern charting, modular strategy development, and open-source extensibility.

Users can upload OHLCV datasets or fetch data from APIs such as Binance and Yahoo Finance, apply indicators, build strategies, and run backtests through a modern web interface.

Tech stack:
• Frontend: Next.js, React, TypeScript, Tailwind CSS
• Backend: FastAPI, Python, WebSockets
• Quant stack: Pandas, NumPy, TA-Lib
• Infrastructure: Docker, PostgreSQL, GitHub Actions

Planned features include candlestick charts, drawing tools, indicator libraries, strategy builder UI, analytics dashboard, and modular plugin support.

gh.description: Open-source GUI-based quantitative backtesting platform for strategy development, market visualization, and collaborative quant research.

projects.name: Arnio
projects.description: Arnio targets the performance bottlenecks found in the "initial ingestion" phase of data science workflows. While Pandas is excellent for analysis, its performance often degrades when handling large, raw CSVs with significant cleaning requirements. Arnio intercepts these files, performs operations like whitespace stripping, case normalization, and null filling natively in C++ via pybind11, and delivers a pristine DataFrame to the user through a zero-copy boundary. It replaces complex .apply() chains with a strict, readable pipeline architecture.

gh.description: C++-accelerated data quality toolkit for Python: clean CSVs, profile messy datasets, validate schemas, and work smoothly with pandas.

projects.name: MergeShip
projects.description:
MergeShip is an innovative open-source management platform designed to bridge the gap between overwhelmed maintainers and novice contributors. We transform the chaotic contribution process into a structured, gamified journey where users are assigned levels (0–3) based on their GitHub history.

Newcomers begin with a five-day foundational course to master Git and open-source etiquette, while experienced developers are placed at intermediate levels. This ensures contributors tackle issues suited to their skills, progressing from documentation to complex features as they earn points and badges.

A key highlight is our hierarchical peer mentorship model: higher-level contributors review the work of lower-level peers. This ensures all PRs reaching maintainers are "Mentor Verified," filtering out low-quality or AI-generated spam. MergeShip provides maintainers with a single dashboard to manage trust-leveled issues, reducing burnout and fostering a sustainable, high-quality ecosystem.

gh.description: MergeShip: a gamified open-source bridge. Contributors find issues via swipe-based discovery, earning XP and tiered badges. Maintainers get an AI Command Center with triage, duplicate detection, and health metrics to prevent burnout and boost PR velocity. Scalable, efficient, and actually fun it's the mission-critical hub for repos.

projects.name: Ai-council-for-enterprise
projects.description: Most AI tools give you one answer, one perspective — and call it a day. But real business decisions touch finance, legal, brand, and strategy all at once, and no single model handles that well. That's what AI Enterprise Council is built to fix. Instead of one AI giving you a confident-sounding response, you get five specialist agents — CEO, CFO, Legal, Marketing, and PR — who actually debate the problem before reaching a conclusion. Each agent pulls context through a RAG pipeline, forms its own stance, and pushes back on the others, with sycophancy guards so they don't just agree to agree. Once the debate settles, a credibility-weighted vote decides the final call. It's designed to feel less like querying an AI and more like walking into a boardroom where everyone actually disagrees.

projects.name: ArduPilot AI Log Diagnosis
projects.description:
An end-to-end AI diagnostic pipeline for ArduPilot .BIN dataflash logs. Drop a crash log and get an instant, physics-grounded root-cause diagnosis with confidence scores, causal timelines, 3D flight replay, and actionable repair recommendations.

The core innovation is CITA (Crash-Immune Temporal Arbitration) — it eliminates the #1 problem in automated crash analysis: post-crash noise misdiagnosed as root cause. The hybrid engine fuses a rule-based system (13 deterministic checks) with a calibrated XGBoost classifier trained on 140+ real crash logs, achieving Macro F1: 1.00 with < 350ms inference per log.

Contributors can work on ML model improvements, new diagnosis rules, CLI tooling, dashboard features, REST API, or dataset pipeline tooling — making it accessible to all skill levels.

gh.description: ML-powered ArduPilot flight log analyzer — classifies anomalies, detects GPS/IMU faults, and generates diagnostic reports from .bin logs

projects.name: EnvForge
projects.description:
EnvForge is an MLOps platform built to eliminate AI "dependency hell." Setting up local machine learning environments is notoriously difficult due to clashing dependencies between PyTorch, CUDA, and hardware drivers. EnvForge solves this by generating 100% deterministic, hardware-aware setup scripts that "just work" on the first try.

Designed for AI researchers and software engineers, it saves hours of frustrating GPU debugging. 

Technically, it utilizes a high-performance Python/FastAPI backend connected to a PostgreSQL database, which stores a strictly validated hardware compatibility matrix. The user interface is a premium Next.js web application. Finally, an integrated AI layer uses LLMs (like Ollama) to diagnose local hardware logs and generate safe, precise repair commands.

projects.name: BiasLens
projects.description: BiasLens addresses the critical issue of algorithmic discrimination by serving as an automated "cyber-x-ray" for datasets. Designed for data scientists and ML engineers, it solves the problem of hidden prejudices in training data—such as those affecting hiring or loan approvals—before they are baked into production models. Our technical approach utilizes a high-performance FastAPI backend to orchestrate a fairness engine powered by IBM AIF360 and Fairlearn, computing nine industry-standard metrics like Disparate Impact and the Theil Index. To ensure maximum privacy, the system employs a Zero-Storage architecture, processing data entirely in RAM to remain GDPR-compliant. Furthermore, we integrate Gemini AI via NVIDIA NIM to translate complex statistical audits into actionable, human-readable executive summaries and mitigation strategies, all presented through an immersive Three.js and Chart.js dashboard.

gh.description: Bringing transparency to data. BiasLens provides a suite of analytics to identify socio-economic and gender biases in large datasets.

projects.name: CamouChat
projects.description:
CamouChat is an open-source, modular Python framework for high-stealth messaging platform automation. It enables developers to build human-like automation on messaging platforms (starting with WhatsApp Web) without relying on official APIs — using a hybrid approach that combines internal JavaScript RAM-function calls with real browser DOM interaction signals to pass bot-detection systems.

The project is structured as a workspace of focused plugins:

- camouchat-core — shared protocols, AES-256-GCM encryption, and key management
- camouchat-browser — fingerprint-spoofed browser lifecycle and profile management
- camouchat-whatsapp — WhatsApp automation with event hooks, encrypted storage, and humanized interaction telemetry
CamouChat is async-first, fully typed, and built with clean architecture principles (Repository Pattern, decorator-based middleware, per-profile session isolation). It is designed to be extensible — contributors can add new platform plugins, storage backends, or hook middleware without touching the core.

Good first issues include: writing tests, adding new query methods, building new platform plugins, improving documentation, and contributing CI/CD workflows.

gh.description: CamouChat : A stealth-first Python SDK for cross-platform messaging automation. Built with async pipelines, database queues, and anti-detect browser routing (Camoufox) to safely automate web messengers like WhatsApp without session bans.If you like this repo, make sure to star!!!

projects.name: EyeGuard — AI Vision Health & Digital Correction System
projects.description:
EyeGuard is a privacy-first Chrome extension that monitors eye 
health in real time using on-device computer vision. It detects 
screen distance, blink rate, and ambient lighting via MediaPipe 
FaceMesh (468 landmarks, ~100fps), scores daily eye health 0-100 
across 4 metrics, and applies software-level visual correction 
(contrast, blue light, font scaling) to any webpage.

The full pipeline runs entirely on-device — no camera frames or 
landmarks are ever stored or transmitted. Only scalar metrics 
(distanceCm, blinkRate, lux) reach IndexedDB. A React dashboard 
shows 30-day trends, a weighted linear regression prediction 
engine forecasts 7-30 day eye strain risk, and a smart alert 
system fires non-intrusive nudges (20-20-20 rule, distance 
warnings, blink reminders) with cooldown and anti-annoyance caps.

gh.description: Your personal eye health guardian that protects your vision while you work.

projects.name: AI Slop Guardian
projects.description:
The curl maintainer reported that 20%+ of bug reports are now AI-generated noise. Excalidraw saw their PR volume double in a single quarter, most of it from people who "vibe coded" a fix without understanding the codebase. Maintainers are burning out.

There was no tool that could actually detect this at the repo level. So I built one.

How it works:

When a PR is opened, Guardian runs 4 detectors in parallel:
→ GPT-2 perplexity scoring (AI text is statistically predictable)
→ Embedding similarity against YOUR repo's own writing style
→ Pattern matching for 20+ AI tell-tale phrases
→ Sentence burstiness analysis

If the score is high, it:
1. Labels the PR automatically (ai-slop:high/medium/low)
2. Posts 3 comprehension questions only the real author could answer
3. Writes a draft code review for the maintainer
4. Detects and rate-limits PR flood attacks

gh.description: GitHub App to detect and label AI-generated slop in PRs, issues, and comments

projects.name: Telos Runtime
projects.description:
Telos Runtime closes the security gap in autonomous AI agent deployments. When an LLM agent executes tool calls, nothing prevents it from reading sensitive files and exfiltrating data over the network, even in memory-safe code. Telos solves this at the kernel level using eBPF/LSM hooks.

Agents declare intent in natural language. Telos translates this to kernel policy via the Cortex AI plane and enforces it at Ring 0. If an agent reads /etc/shadow and attempts a network connection, the 
kernel permanently slams the network gate before a single byte leaves the machine.

Contributors can work on: LangChain/AutoGPT agent demos, domain intelligence rules, Prometheus 
dashboards, Dockerfile, benchmark suite vs Docker and gVisor, and Pipelock MCP integration.

gh.description: A closed-loop security runtime preventing "The Great Exfiltration" and Indirect Prompt Injection in Autonomous AI Agents.

projects.name: Eagle:-Real-Time Semantic Surveillance using Detection + Tracking + Reasoning

"A person is loitering near the restricted exit and repeatedly looking at the keypad."

Core Value Proposition: Traditional CV detects → Agentic Vision understands. It fuses YOLO-based perception, ByteTrack identity tracking, Redis temporal memory, and a Vision-Language Model (VLM) reasoning layer into a single agentic pipeline.

-> What Makes This "2026-Level"?
- Zero-shot behavior detection — no explicit training required for every    behavior type
- Temporal semantic reasoning across 10–30 second activity windows
Natural language explanations instead of raw alert codes
- Agentic decision loop: perceive → remember → reason → alert → adapt
- Human-in-the-loop feedback for continuous model improvement

projects.name: Humane-Proxy
projects.description:
HumaneProxy is a lightweight, privacy-first AI safety middleware designed to protect humans. While most AI safety tools focus on preventing prompt injections, HumaneProxy specifically detects and intercepts self-harm ideation and criminal intent before the message reaches the upstream LLM.

Technical Approach: It operates as a plug-and-play reverse proxy and MCP server using a highly efficient 3-stage cascading pipeline:

1. Heuristics & Regex (instant blocking)
2. Semantic Embeddings (sentence-transformers)
3. Reasoning LLMs (LlamaGuard/OpenAI).

It features exponential time-decay risk tracking, webhook alerts, and native LangChain/LlamaIndex and other integrations. It is AAA rated on Glama MCP marketplace and is actively in development.

Who is it for: AI developers and enterprises wanting to add production-grade trust & safety guardrails to mitigate societal damage risks with their AI agents without altering their core LLM logic or compromising user privacy.

gh.description: Lightweight AI safety middleware that protects humans by intercepting self-harm and criminal intent in LLM prompts. Features a 3-stage safety pipeline, MCP server for agents, and automated care responses.

projects.name: urBackend - From Clicks to Secure APIs in seconds.
projects.description:
urBackend is an open-source, MongoDB-native Backend-as-a-Service (BaaS) platform that gives frontend developers instant REST APIs for database, authentication, file storage, transactional mail, and real-time features - without writing a single line of backend code.

Unlike Firebase (closed-source, vendor lock-in) or Supabase (Postgres-only), urBackend is built specifically for the MongoDB ecosystem. Its core philosophy is BYOK (Bring Your Own Keys) - developers can connect their own MongoDB Atlas cluster, and S3/Cloudflare R2 storage bucket — with Resend mail, FCM push, and SMS integrations actively in development.

The project is actively maintained with weekly releases (v0.4 → v0.6 shipped in one week), a live production API at api.ub.bitbros.in and live at https://urbackend.bitbros.in, an official TypeScript SDK on npm (@urbackend/sdk), and a growing contributor base across TypeScript, Java, and Python.

For GSSoC contributors, urBackend offers a wide range of meaningful tasks across the full stack:
• Backend (Node.js/Express) - new API features, security improvements, webhook system
• Frontend (React/Vite) - dashboard UI, schema builder, analytics visualizations
• SDK (TypeScript) - expanding @urbackend/sdk with React hooks, new methods
• Java SDK - building urBackend client library for Android and Spring Boot developers (Future Scope )
• Python — analytics microservice, data pipeline for usage insights(Future)
• Documentation — API reference, guides, getting started tutorials
• Testing — integration test suite for production endpoints

Contributors will work on a real, production-deployed product used by developers - not a toy project. Every merged PR ships to actual users.

urBackend is MIT licensed, open source on GitHub, and welcomes contributors of all experience levels — from first-time open source contributors to experienced developers looking to build meaningful features.

gh.description: urBackend: The open-source backend for high-growth apps. Effortlessly generate secure endpoints with native Auth, RLS, and BYO-Database support. Stop building boilerplate, start building features.

projects.name: Harmonic-Resonance-Forest
projects.description:
I developed Harmonic Resonance Fields (HRF), a novel physics-informed machine learning algorithm that achieved 98.84% accuracy on the EEG Eye State Corpus (OpenML ID: 1471), surpassing all industry-standard models, including Random Forest, XGBoost, and Extra Trees.

What makes this significant:

v15.0 Upgrade: Now powered by NVIDIA RAPIDS (cuML & CuPy) for parallel resonance calculations.
Statistically Proven: Validated via 5-Fold Stratified Cross-Validation with negligible variance (±0.18%).
Physics-Based: Models classification as wave interference rather than statistical splitting.
Robust: Validated on 14,980 real-world medical samples with superior phase-jitter resistance.

gh.description: Harmonic Resonance Fields (HRF): a mathematically derived, physics-inspired classification framework that models data interactions via spectral resonance rather than geometric decision boundaries.

projects.name: SQL-Based Data Warehouse for Retail Analytics with BI Integration
projects.description: This project addresses the challenge of transforming raw, inconsistent data from sources like CRM and ERP systems into a structured and reliable format suitable for analytics. It is designed for business analysts, data analysts, and organizations that need accurate insights from retail sales data, as well as for learners aiming to understand real-world data engineering workflows. The technical approach follows the Medallion Architecture (Bronze → Silver → Gold), where raw data is first ingested into the Bronze layer, then cleaned, standardized, and validated in the Silver layer using SQL transformations, and finally modeled into a star schema in the Gold layer for efficient querying. The pipeline is implemented using SQL-based ETL processes and is connected to Power BI to generate interactive dashboards, enabling data-driven decision-making.

GameHub is an elite, open-source collection of classic and modern browser games. Re-imagined with a Cosmic Blue Neon aesthetic, it combines the nostalgia of retro gaming with the performance of industry-standard web tech.

Originally a Vanilla JS project, GameHub has been upgraded to a React + Django REST Framework hybrid architecture supporting massive scalability, premium animations, a global leaderboard system, full JWT authentication, Google OAuth 2.0, PWA installability, and a password reset flow.

gh.description: GameHub is a free, open-source collection of classic browser games designed to bring nostalgia, fun, and learning together in one place. Built with modern web technologies and a focus on simplicity, GameHub offers smooth gameplay, responsive design, and clean code that makes it easy for developers to explore, learn, and contribute.

projects.name: AccountSafe
projects.description: AccountSafe self-hosted, zero-knowledge credential manager. It encrypts all sensitive data in the browser before transmission. The server stores only ciphertext. A compromised database yields no usable information.

gh.description: A self-hosted, zero-knowledge credential manager.

projects.name: Car Transport Service
projects.description:
The Car Transport Service Project is designed to streamline and manage the process of vehicle transportation from one location to another efficiently and safely. The system focuses on handling customer requests, scheduling vehicle pickups and deliveries, and tracking the movement of cars throughout the transportation process.

As part of the project, responsibilities include managing client data, coordinating with drivers and logistics teams, maintaining transport records, and ensuring timely delivery of vehicles. The project also emphasizes route planning, real-time tracking, and effective communication to enhance customer satisfaction.

The goal of this project is to improve operational efficiency, reduce delays, and provide a reliable and organized car transport service through proper planning, monitoring, and management.

gh.description: Beginner-friendly Car Transport Service. Open for contributions to improve design, features, and functionality. projects.name: Foodgram
projects.description: FoodGram is a MERN-stack video sharing platform addressing the problem of unreliable food discovery. Traditional static reviews often fail to capture a dish's true portion, texture, or a restaurant's atmosphere, leading to consumer disappointment. By prioritizing short form reels, FoodGram provides an authentic "vibe check" that static photos cannot match. Built with MongoDB, Express, React, and Node.js, it creates a dedicated, high-performance ecosystem where users can visually verify culinary experiences before visiting, ensuring discovery is both trustworthy and immersive.

gh.description: A social video platform for food lovers. Share and discover food reels from local partners. Built with React, Node.js, and MongoDB.

projects.name: BizInsight-AI
projects.description: BizInsight AI is an AI-powered customer intelligence platform designed to help businesses transform raw customer reviews into actionable growth strategies. The platform solves the problem of manual data overload by automatically analyzing feedback CSV files to detect customer sentiment, track satisfaction trends over time, and identify recurring pain points using keyword extraction. It is specifically built for e-commerce owners, service providers, and business analysts who need to monitor product quality and customer experience without sorting through thousands of comments manually. The technical approach involves a Python-based web dashboard created with Streamlit, which uses TextBlob for sentiment scoring and SQLite for persistent data storage. A key feature is the integration of the DeepSeek AI model, which acts as a virtual business consultant to answer specific questions about customer patterns and suggest concrete improvement actions.

gh.description: BizInsight AI is an AI-powered platform that converts customer reviews into actionable insights using sentiment analysis, issue detection, trend tracking, and intelligent recommendations,all in one dashboard.

projects.name: EventOne
projects.description:
EventOne is a full-stack event management platform designed to streamline the entire lifecycle of events—from discovery to participation and management.

It solves the problem of fragmented event systems by providing a single platform for organizers, attendees, and admins. Attendees can discover events, register instantly, and receive QR-based digital tickets, while organizers can manage events, track participants, and perform real-time check-ins. Admins ensure platform quality through moderation and analytics.

The technical approach is based on a modern full-stack architecture, using a RESTful backend with real-time communication (Socket.IO) and a responsive frontend. The system implements role-based access control, JWT authentication, real-time event handling, and PDF ticket generation, making it production-ready and scalable.

gh.description: Full-stack event management system with QR ticketing, real-time check-ins, organizer dashboards, and role-based access. Stack: Node.js · React · MongoDB · Socket.IO · Tailwind CSS

projects.name: NexaSphere
projects.description: As part of NexaSphere, I developed the club’s website to create a centralized platform for sharing updates, resources, events, and learning opportunities with students. The website helps streamline communication within the community, showcase technical initiatives, and improve accessibility to workshops and project activities. This project supported building a stronger student developer ecosystem and encouraged more structured participation in cloud and emerging technology learning initiatives.

projects.name: SkillsSphere-AI
projects.description:
SkillSphere AI is an intelligent full-stack platform designed to bridge the gap between learning and career readiness. It enables tutors to conduct live interactive classes while empowering students with AI-driven tools to improve their skills, optimize their resumes, and prepare for real-world job opportunities.

The platform integrates live classrooms, resume analysis, job description matching, and AI-based mock interviews into a single ecosystem. Using advanced machine learning and generative AI, SkillSphere AI analyzes user profiles, identifies skill gaps, and provides personalized recommendations to help learners become job-ready.

Unlike traditional EdTech platforms, SkillSphere AI goes beyond just teaching — it focuses on end-to-end growth, from learning concepts to getting hired. It also supports recruiters by providing data-driven insights into candidate skills and performance.

By combining education, AI, and hiring workflows, SkillSphere AI aims to create a smarter, more efficient way for students to learn, improve, and succeed in their careers.

gh.description: SkillSphere AI is an AI-powered platform for live learning, resume analysis, and job readiness. Tutors host interactive classes, while students improve skills, match resumes with job descriptions, and prepare through AI mock interviews—bridging the gap between learning and hiring.
